notes: Both configurations scored 100% pass rate — the assertions cover format compliance and zone correctness but don't capture qualitative differences between skill versions.

Key qualitative difference on eval-1 (mixed-emotions): new skill selected #mood-drained (emotional exhaustion from sleeplessness+workload) while old skill selected #mood-unsettled (unresolved feeling). New skill's WHY paragraph about 1-tag precision likely guided a more specific choice.
New skill consistently uses ~1565 more tokens on average — the added edge-case guidance in วิธีวิเคราะห์ section is being read and applied.
New skill is ~9.6s slower on average — acceptable overhead given richer context about when to use single dominant signal vs holistic read.
All 3 evals showed consistent zone assignment: Yellow for unambiguous positive/high-energy, Blue for negative/low-energy — both versions handled zone boundaries correctly.
